title: Windows Suspiciously Long Command Line (Field Analysis)
description: Flags processes with unusually long command lines (>500 chars). Attackers frequently use long command lines for encoded payloads, obfuscated scripts, and base64-encoded commands. Uses eval-based field creation (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Searching/Filtering method with field creation).
query: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs' | eval cmd_len = length('Command Line') | where cmd_len > 500 | sort -cmd_len | fields 'Process Name', 'Command Line', cmd_len, 'Host Name', User
falsepositives: Legitimate PowerShell scripts with many parameters
Deployment tools with long argument lists
